channel.title: Boldly Balance
channel.description: Science-backed wellness, lifestyle, and personal growth. Practical strategies for living well in a connected world.

item.title: Burnout Recovery: The Evidence-Based Guide to Getting Yourself Back
item.description: Burnout isn't just exhaustion — it's a specific syndrome with distinct stages. Here's how to recognize it, recover from it, and build genuine resistance.

item.title: The Gratitude Practice That Actually Works (And Why Most People Do It Wrong)
item.description: Gratitude lists have become a cliché. But the science is real — with important caveats about HOW to practice gratitude effectively.

item.title: Journaling: The Science-Backed Guide to Why It Works
item.description: Journaling isn't just a wellness trend. Research shows it can reduce anxiety, improve immune function, and clarify thinking. Here's how to do it in a way that actually sticks.

item.title: The Art of Boundaries: A Practical Guide to Protecting Your Time and Energy
item.description: Saying no is a skill. Here's how to set boundaries at work, in relationships, and with yourself — backed by psychology and practical frameworks that actually work.

item.title: Omega-3: The Complete Guide to Dosage, Sources, and What Actually Works
item.description: Fish oil, algae omega-3, DHA, EPA — the omega-3 space is confusing. Here's what the science says about dosage, benefits, and how to choose a supplement.

item.title: Intermittent Fasting: What the Science Actually Says
item.description: 16:8, 5:2, OMAD — everyone has an opinion on intermittent fasting. Here's what the research actually supports.

item.title: Gut Health: What Actually Matters (And What's Just Noise)
item.description: Probiotics, prebiotics, fermented foods, gut-brain axis. The gut health space is loud. Here's what the science actually supports — and what you can skip.

item.title: The Cold Plunge: What the Science Actually Says
item.description: Cold plunges are everywhere. But does the science back the hype? Here's what research actually shows about cold water exposure, recovery, mood, and longevity.

item.title: Sleep Hacks That Actually Work (And the Ones That Don't)
item.description: Most sleep advice is garbage. Here's what actually works: science-backed sleep hacks for falling asleep faster, sleeping deeper, and waking up refreshed. No fluff.

item.title: Why Your Phone's Default Settings Are Sabotaging Your Productivity
item.description: Your phone comes preconfigured to fragment your attention. Here's exactly what to change—and why the defaults aren't neutral.

item.title: The AI Tool That Actually Saved Me Time (After Three That Didn't)
item.description: I tried three AI productivity tools that promised to save hours. Only one delivered. Here's what actually worked and why the others failed.

item.title: Why Productivity Apps Make You Less Productive — And What to Do Instead
item.description: You have Notion for planning, Todoist for tasks, Toggl for tracking, Forest for focus, and a Pomodoro timer running in the background. You have spent more time organizing your productivity system than actually being productive.

item.title: Why Constant Connectivity Is Rewiring Your Brain
item.description: Always online? Research shows constant connectivity is changing how your brain works. Learn the effects and practical ways to reclaim your focus.

item.title: The Aesthetic Bedroom Trend That's Costing You Hours of Sleep
item.description: Your Instagram-worthy bedroom might be sabotaging your sleep. From LED strips to all-black interiors, here's how aesthetics clash with recovery.

item.title: Why Your Productivity App Is Making You Less Productive
item.description: Productivity apps promise to save time but often waste it. Here is why the tool you rely on might be your biggest distraction — and what actually works instead.

item.title: Why Digital Detoxes Fail — And the Tech Habits That Actually Stick
item.description: Digital detoxes promise freedom but deliver rebound binges. Here is why cold turkey does not work and what sustainable tech habits look like instead.

item.title: Why "Staying Positive" Is Making You More Anxious
item.description: Forcing yourself to think positive thoughts doesn't reduce anxiety—it adds a second layer of failure. Here's why toxic positivity backfires and what actually works instead.

item.title: Why "Staying Positive" Is Making You More Anxious
item.description: Forcing positivity can amplify anxiety instead of reducing it. Learn why toxic positivity backfires and what actually works for emotional resilience.

item.title: Why Overthinking Isn't Actually a Thinking Problem
item.description: Overthinking isn't a logic failure — it's an emotion regulation problem. Here's why that distinction matters and what actually helps you stop the spiral.

item.title: The 50/30/20 Rule Is Dead: A Realistic Budget Framework for 2026
item.description: The 50/30/20 budget rule was designed for a world that no longer exists. Here's a modern framework that actually works when rent eats half your income.
